rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or the input AADL model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure that the inserted AGREE annex blocks do not introduce new identifiers or references that are not visible in the current scope
Preserve the existing AADL property associations and do not encode them as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed in the model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Omit event-port activation clauses unless the model provides an explicit data value for the event
anti_patterns: Do not remove or modify existing AADL architecture elements during fusion
Avoid using AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit exposure in the model
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly provided in the model
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
